title: Podman uses incorrect path to auth.json
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

After https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3760/files , podman _always_ hard-codes `SystemContext.AuthFilePath` to a non-empty value, overriding the built-in defaults in c/image — but _different_ from the built-in defaults.

E.g. for the ordinary case of an unprivileged user with `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` set, the c/image default is `/run/user/`_$UID_`/containers/auth.json`, while the Podman default is `/run/user/`_$UID_`/auth.json`.

This breaks `podman login`+`skopeo`, as well as Skopeo integration tests, and presumably any other non-Podman software that relies on `podman login`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman login $registry`
2. `skopeo inspect docker://$registry/$image`
(or use `strace -e 'trace=%file' … |& grep auth` to confirm)

**Describe the results you received:**

In Skopeo debug output,
```
level=debug msg="Credentials not found"
```
\+ a failure to read data.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful image use.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This does not show up as a Podman failure as long as Podman is consuming its own `podman login` effects because both the writer and the readers use the same incorrect path.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

podman-1.6.1-2.fc30 as well as current master ( f61e399a66c716cc6b18659e6b21208007d48848 ).

comments.body: Thanks for the detailed explanation, @mtrmac! We need to check when we regressed on that and maybe need to backport the fix (Cc: @mheon). It's unlikely that I will find time to look into this today. If others want to pick it up, feel free.
